macro_events.event_summary: Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari said the ongoing Iran war is constraining the Fed's ability to provide forward guidance on rates, signaling prolonged policy uncertainty amid a geopolitical oil shock.
primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk premium on crude oil from Iran conflict
Fed policy uncertainty / delayed rate cut path keeping real yields elevated
Flight-to-safety bid for defense and gold

beneficiaries.rationale: Iran war sustains crude price risk premium; ExxonMobil's integrated upstream earnings lever directly to Brent. Middle East supply disruption + no US production exposure to Iran = pure beneficiary of higher oil.
Pure-play E&P with high oil beta and no refining drag; every $5 sustained move in Brent flows largely to FCF given current hedge book. Geopolitical premium directly accretive.
Iran war drives sustained munitions replenishment demand (PAC-3, THAAD, Javelin) and allied FMS orders. Direct revenue exposure to Middle East conflict via missile defense franchises.
Patriot/SM-3/Tomahawk operator — actively expended in Middle East theater, requiring restock orders. Raytheon segment is the most direct beneficiary of prolonged Iran kinetic activity.
Fed unable to guide on cuts + war risk = bid for gold as inflation/geopolitical hedge. Newmont is the largest-cap pure gold miner with high operating leverage to spot price.
Permian-weighted E&P with high oil-price torque and strong FCF conversion; Buffett-backed name often rotates as a US-domestic oil-spike trade with no Iran/Russia counterparty risk.
Northrop's missile defense and B-21/long-range strike portfolio are directly relevant to a sustained Iran campaign; budget supplemental risk skews up.

losers.rationale: Higher sustained jet fuel from Iran war + no near-term Fed relief on demand-side rates = direct margin compression. Delta has the highest international exposure among US carriers, with rerouted Middle East/Asia flights adding fuel burn.
Same fuel-cost transmission as DAL with even greater long-haul international mix; Middle East airspace closures force costly reroutes on India/Asia routes.
Southwest is unhedged on fuel and runs thinner margins; a sustained crude spike with no Fed cut cushion hits earnings directly.
Cruise operator with direct fuel cost exposure AND Mediterranean/Middle East itinerary cancellation risk from Iran war escalation. Double hit.
Air freight margins compress on jet fuel spike; FedEx Express is the most fuel-exposed segment in logistics and lacks pricing power in current freight recession.

headlines: Kashkari says Iran war limits Fed's ability to provide rate guidance
